name: The 3 Closes Follow-Up Funnel (Emotion → Logic → Fear)
source_books: DotCom Secrets (Russell Brunson) — Follow-Up Funnels + 3 Closes
Marketing Secrets Blackbook — customized follow-up funnels, urgency/scarcity, Stack Don't Switch
Lead Funnels Swipe File — Follow-Up Funnels (multi-channel)
Copywriting Secrets (Edwards) — PAS, Before/After/Bridge, proof, CTA
framework: The master close architecture. Brunson: a single offer is sold THREE different ways in sequence because different buyers respond to different closes. Emotion converts the emotionally-driven now; Logic converts the analytical justifier; Fear/Urgency converts the procrastinator at the deadline.

trigger.entry_conditions: Has email; has an active offer to close; not already a buyer of THIS offer.
trigger.exit_conditions: Purchase of the offer → buyer onboarding + next rung of value ladder. End of Fear close with no buy → drop to long-term Seinfeld nurture.

cadence: 30–60 days across the value ladder. Phase 1 Emotion (days 0–5), Phase 2 Logic (days 6–12), Phase 3 Fear/Urgency (final 24–72h window).

copy_persona.primary: Russell Brunson — 3 Closes + Attractive Character + Stack
supporting: Jim Edwards — Problem/Agitate/Solve, Before/After/Bridge, the 13-part offer block, proof, single CTA

close_modules.emotion: Story-driven. Run the 5-email Soap Opera Sequence (see soap-opera-sequence template) OR a single Epiphany Bridge story email. Goal: make them WANT it.
close_modules.logic: Justify the purchase rationally so the buyer (and their spouse/boss) can say yes: the math/ROI, the Stack of deliverables and their value ('dollars for dimes'), feature-benefit-meaning bullets, comparison vs. alternatives, the guarantee removing risk. Goal: make saying yes the SMART choice.
close_modules.fear: Urgency + scarcity. What they LOSE by not acting: cost of inaction, expiring bonus, closing cart, limited spots, price increase. Goal: make NOW the only logical time.

sequence.beat: Run the Soap Opera Sequence (Set Stage → Drama/Wall → Epiphany → Hidden Benefits → Urgency/CTA).
Recap the transformation and pivot: 'Let me show you exactly what you get and why it pays for itself.'
sequence.purpose: Hand off from heart to head.

sequence.beat: THE STACK as logic: list every deliverable with its standalone value, accumulate the total, then reveal the (much lower) price. 'Dollars for dimes.'
sequence.body_framework: Stack each component + value → cumulative total → price reveal → 'which means…' meaning on each item
sequence.persona_formula: Brunson Stack + Edwards Ultimate Bullet Formula

sequence.beat: ROI / cost-of-inaction math + comparison vs. the alternatives (DIY, do-nothing, competitor method — never named).
sequence.purpose: Make yes the rational choice; pre-empt 'too expensive'.
sequence.subject_angle: 'The math nobody does (it's cheaper to buy than to wait)'
sequence.persona_formula: Edwards Problem/Agitate/Solve on cost-of-inaction; good-vs-bad negative

sequence.beat: Proof + guarantee: testimonials, case studies, data, and the risk-reversal guarantee. Address top 2–3 objections head-on (FAQ).
sequence.subject_angle: 'Still on the fence? Read this (then decide)'
sequence.persona_formula: Edwards proof + guarantee block; Brunson objection/false-belief handling

sequence.beat: Announce the deadline/closing window. 'This is the part where it gets time-sensitive.'
48-hour warning. Restate the offer + the three scarcity levers (limited time / price / quantity).
24-hour / fast-action bonus expiring. Cost-of-inaction reminder (what they lose).
Final hours: 'doors closing,' last-call. Two short emails (3h, 1h) + SMS at 1h.
sequence.purpose: Final conversion spike (largest single revenue beat in most campaigns).

sequence.beat: Cart closed. Buyers → onboarding + ascend value ladder. Non-buyers → drop to Seinfeld nurture; optional one-time downsell.
sequence.purpose: Route everyone cleanly; honor the deadline (cart truly closes).

ghl_build.trigger: Workflow Trigger: 'Tag Added' = 'start-3closes-[offer]' (set by opt-in, webinar-attended, or Seinfeld interest click). Filter: not tagged 'buyer-[offer]'.
actions: PHASE 1 EMOTION: call/embed the Soap Opera Sequence steps (Day 0–4).
Wait → PHASE 2 LOGIC: Stack email (Day 6), ROI/comparison email (Day 8), Proof+Guarantee email (Day 10), each with Buy CTA.
Wait → PHASE 3 FEAR: deadline announce (Day 12), T-48h, T-24h, T-3h, T-1h — emails + SMS, using a real countdown.
Multi-channel mirrors: GHL Facebook/IG message action on Stack + Final-Call beats; desktop push on Day 6 and T-24h; sync a retargeting audience for ad reinforcement.
wait_steps: Use 'Wait until' fixed campaign dates for the Fear phase so the deadline is REAL and shared across all channels (countdown timer on the sales page must match).
if_else: PURCHASE GOAL ('buyer-[offer]') at ANY step → exit, enroll buyer onboarding + next value-ladder offer (Stack Don't Switch), STOP all close emails.

Branch on Logic-phase engagement: IF opened Stack email but no click → resend with new subject + a single hero benefit. IF clicked sales page 2+ times no buy → tag 'hot-no-buy' and prioritize SMS in Fear phase.
Branch on Fear phase: IF clicked checkout but did not complete → fire 'abandoned-cart-recovery' workflow immediately (overrides scheduling).
Post-close branch: buyers vs non-buyers (tag-based). Non-buyers → optional 'downsell' workflow once, then 'Seinfeld Daily Sequence'.
ghl_build.notes: GoHighLevel: build as ONE Workflow with three labeled sections (Emotion / Logic / Fear). Drive the Fear phase off a fixed campaign deadline custom field so the on-page countdown timer (sales/checkout page) and the emails/SMS all reference the SAME moment. The Purchase Goal must be wired to short-circuit from any step. Use the GHL multi-channel actions (Email, SMS, FB/IG DM, Web Push) to replicate Brunson's multi-channel follow-up funnel. HONOR the deadline — when the cart closes in GHL, the offer/price genuinely changes.

integrity_guardrails: Each close sells the SAME true offer three ways — never inflate claims to fit a close.
The Stack values must be defensible (real standalone value), not fabricated (Edwards proof rule + dollars-for-dimes done honestly).
The Fear-phase deadline MUST be honored — cart closes for real, bonus truly expires (Edwards Secret #18; Blackbook urgency must be real or it destroys trust).
kpis: Conversion by phase (which close converts each buyer)
Fear-phase revenue concentration (typically the largest beat)
